NEW ISSUES - BOOK-ENTRY ONLY RATINGS: See "RATINGS" herein
In the opinion of Co-Bond Counsel, under current law and subject to conditions described herein under the caption "TAX MATTERS," (a) interest on the Series 2025A 
Bonds (i) is not included in gross income for federal income tax purposes, and (ii) is not an item of tax preference for purposes of the federal alternative minimum income tax; 
(b) interest on the Series 2025B Bonds (i) is not included in gross income for federal income tax purposes except when held by a "substantial user" of the Airport facilities or a 
"related person" of a "substantial user" within the meaning of Section 147(a) of the Code, and (ii) is an item of tax preference for purposes of the federal alternative minimum 
income tax; (c) interest on the Series 2025 Bonds is taken into account by applicable corporations (as defined in Section 59(k) of the Code) for the alternative minimum tax 
imposed on such corporations; and (d) interest on the Series 2025 Bonds is exempt from income taxation by the State of Georgia.  A holder may be subject to other federal tax 
consequences as described under the caption "TAX MATTERS."  See the proposed forms of the approving opinions of Co-Bond Counsel in APPENDIX F hereto.

REVENUE BONDS,
SERIES 2025B-2 (AMT)
Dated: Date of Delivery Due: July 1, as shown on the inside front cover page
This Official Statement relates to the issuance and sale by the City of Atlanta (the "City") of $49,490,000 in aggregate principal amount of its Airport General Revenue Bonds, 
Series 2025A (Non‑AMT) (the "Series 2025A Bonds"), $914,640,000 in aggregate principal amount of its Airport General Revenue Bonds, Series 2025B‑1 (AMT) (Green Bonds) (the 
"Series 2025B‑1 Green Bonds") and $48,660,000 Airport General Revenue Bonds, Series 2025B‑2 (AMT) (the "Series 2025B‑2 Bonds" and, together with the Series 2025B‑1 Green Bonds, 
the "Series 2025B Bonds").  The Series 2025A Bonds and the Series 2025B Bonds are hereinafter referred to as the "Series 2025 Bonds."  All capitalized terms used herein and not 
otherwise expressly defined herein shall have the meanings assigned thereto in "APPENDIX C ‑ DEFINITIONS OF CERTAIN TERMS" attached hereto.
The City has authorized the issuance of the Series 2025 Bonds pursuant to the Restated and Amended Master Bond Ordinance adopted by the City Council of the City (the "City 
Council") on March 20, 2000, as previously amended and supplemented (the "Master Bond Ordinance"), particularly as supplemented by that certain Thirty‑Seventh Supplemental Bond 
Ordinance adopted by the City Council on June 2, 2025 and approved by the Mayor of the City (the "Mayor") on June 3, 2025, as amended and supplemented by that certain ordinance 
adopted by the City Council and approved by the Mayor on September 10, 2025 (together, the "Thirty‑Seventh Supplemental Bond Ordinance").  The Master Bond Ordinance and the 
Thirty‑Seventh Supplemental Bond Ordinance are hereinafter referred to as the "Bond Ordinance."
The proceeds of the Series 2025 Bonds will be used for the purpose of providing funds to, among other things: (a) finance or refinance the costs of planning, engineering, 
designing, acquiring, equipping and constructing of all or a portion of the 2025 Project (as defined herein), which constitutes a portion of the Capital Plan to 2031 (as defined herein); 
(b) fund deposits to the applicable subaccounts of the Debt Service Reserve Account to meet the Debt Service Reserve Requirement related to the Series 2025 Bonds; (c) pay capitalized 
interest; and (d) pay certain costs of issuance with respect to the Series 2025 Bonds.  See "PLAN OF FINANCE," "ESTIMATED SOURCES AND USES OF FUNDS," "SECURITY AND 
SOURCES OF PAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2025 BONDS ‑ Debt Service Reserve Account," and "CAPITAL PLAN TO 2031" herein and "APPENDIX A ‑ REPORT OF THE AIRPORT 
CONSULTANT ‑ AIRPORT FACILITIES AND CAPITAL PLAN ‑ Capital Improvement Plan ‑ Capital Plan to 2031" attached hereto.
The Series 2025 Bonds will be issued as fully registered bonds, without coupons, in denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple thereof and initially will be registered in the 
name of Cede & Co., as nominee of The Depository Trust Company ("DTC").  Purchases of beneficial ownership interests in the Series 2025 Bonds will be made in book‑entry form only 
and purchasers will not receive physical delivery of bond certificates representing the beneficial ownership interests in the Series 2025 Bonds so purchased.  Payments of principal of, 
premium, if any, and interest on, any Series 2025 Bond will be made to Cede & Co., as nominee for DTC as registered owner of the Series 2025 Bonds, by U.S. Bank Trust Company, 
National Association, as bond registrar and paying agent, to be subsequently disbursed to the Beneficial Owners (as defined in APPENDIX H attached hereto) of the Series 2025 Bonds.  
See "APPENDIX H ‑ DTC AND THE BOOK‑ENTRY ONLY SYSTEM" attached hereto.
Interest on the Series 2025 Bonds is payable semiannually on January 1 and July 1 of each year, commencing January 1, 2026.  The Series 2025 Bonds will bear interest at the 
rates and will be payable as to principal in the amounts and on the dates set forth on the inside front cover page of this Official Statement.  See "DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2025 
BONDS ‑ General" herein.
Certain of the Series 2025 Bonds may be subject to redemption prior to maturity as more fully described under the caption "DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2025 
BONDS ‑ Redemption Provisions" herein.
The Series 2025 Bonds are special limited obligations of the City payable from and secured by a pledge of and Senior Lien on General Revenues on a parity with the Outstanding 
Senior Lien General Revenue Bonds (as defined herein) and any other Additional Bonds issued on a parity with such Outstanding Senior Lien General Revenue Bonds under the Bond 
Ordinance, including, when and if issued, the Planned 2026 ‑2031 General Revenue Bonds (as defined herein).  The Series 2025 Bonds will not be secured by PFC Revenues, 
Special Purpose Revenues or Released Revenues, any of which may be pledged to secure other bonds and obligations under the Bond Ordinance.  See "SECURITY AND 
SOURCES OF PAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2025 BONDS ‑ Series 2025 Bonds; Pledge of Pledged Revenues" and "OUTSTANDING AIRPORT OBLIGATIONS ‑ Senior Lien General 
Revenue Bonds" herein and "APPENDIX D ‑ SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE BOND ORDINANCE" attached hereto.
THE SERIES 2025 BONDS ARE SPECIAL LIMITED OBLIGATIONS OF THE CITY AND SHALL NOT BE DEEMED TO CONSTITUTE A DEBT OF THE CITY 
WITHIN THE MEANING OF ANY CONSTITUTIONAL LIMITATION ON DEBT NOR CONSTITUTE A PLEDGE OF THE FAITH AND CREDIT OF THE CITY.  THE 
SERIES 2025 BONDS SHALL NOT BE PAYABLE FROM OR BE A CHARGE UPON ANY FUNDS OTHER THAN THE REVENUES AND AMOUNTS PLEDGED TO THE 
PAYMENT THEREOF PURSUANT TO THE BOND ORDINANCE, NOR SHALL THE CITY BE SUBJECT TO ANY PECUNIARY LIABILITY THEREON.  NO OWNER OR 
OWNERS OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS SHALL EVER HAVE THE RIGHT TO COMPEL ANY EXERCISE OF THE TAXING POWER OF THE CITY TO PAY THE SERIES 
2025 BONDS OR THE INTEREST THEREON, NOR TO ENFORCE PAYMENT OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS AGAINST ANY PROPERTY OF THE CITY; NOR SHALL 
THE SERIES 2025 BONDS CONSTITUTE A CHARGE, LIEN, OR ENCUMBRANCE, LEGAL OR EQUITABLE, UPON ANY PROPERTY OF THE CITY, EXCEPT FOR THE 
AMOUNTS PLEDGED TO THE PAYMENT OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS AND ANY OTHER FUNDS PLEDGED TO SECURE THE PAYMENT OF THE SERIES 2025 
BONDS IN THE MANNER SET FORTH IN THE BOND ORDINANCE.
The City has designated the Series 2025B‑1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds (as defined herein) based upon, among other things, the Green Bond Framework (as defined herein) and 
the Second Party Opinion (as defined herein) provided by Kestrel, all as more particularly described under "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B‑1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS" 
herein and "APPENDIX I ‑ SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL" attached hereto.
This cover page contains certain limited information for quick reference only.  It is not, and is not intended to be, a summary of the matters relating to the Series 2025 Bonds.  
Potential investors should read the entire Official Statement, including the inside front cover page and the appendices attached hereto, to obtain information essential to the making 
of an informed investment decision.
The Series 2025 Bonds will be offered when, as, and if issued by the City and received by the Underwriters, subject to prior sale and to withdrawal or modification of the 
offer without notice, and subject to the approving opinions of Hunton Andrews Kurth LLP and Johnson & Freeman, LLC, both of Atlanta, Georgia, in their capacity as Co-Bond 
Counsel.  Certain legal matters in connection with the Series 2025 Bonds will be passed upon for the City by the City's Department of Law.  Greenberg Traurig, LLP and Riddle 
& Schwartz, LLC, both of Atlanta, Georgia, have served as Co-Disclosure Counsel in connection with the Series 2025 Bonds.  Certain legal matters in connection with the Series 
2025 Bonds will be passed upon for the Underwriters by Squire Patton Boggs (US) LLP and Tiber Hudson LLC, both of Atlanta, Georgia.  Frasca & Associates, LLC, Atlanta, 
Georgia, is serving as Financial Advisor to the City.  The Series 2025 Bonds are expected to be delivered through the book-entry system of DTC on or about September 24, 2025.

JURISDICTION TO ANY PERSON TO WHOM IT IS UNLAWFUL TO MAKE SUCH AN 
OFFER IN SUCH JURISDICTION.

BY THE CITY OR THE UNDERWRITERS TO GIVE ANY INFORMATION OR TO MAKE 
ANY REPRESENTATIONS, OTHER THAN THOSE CONTAINED IN THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT, IN CONNECTION WITH THE OFFERING OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS, 
AND IF GIVEN OR MADE, SUCH INFORMATION OR REPRESENTATIONS MUST NOT 
BE RELIED UPON AS HAVING BEEN AUTHORIZED BY THE CITY OR ANY OTHER 
PERSON.  THE INFORMATION AND EXPRESSIONS OF OPINION IN THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT ARE SUBJECT TO CHANGE WITHOUT NOTICE, AND THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT SPEAKS ONLY AS OF ITS DATE.  NEITHER THE DELIVERY OF THIS 
OFFICIAL STATEMENT NOR ANY SALE MADE HEREUNDER WILL, UNDER ANY 
CIRCUMSTANCES, CREATE THE IMPLICATION THAT THERE HAS BEEN NO CHANGE 
IN THE MATTERS DESCRIBED HEREIN SINCE THE DATE HEREOF.  EXCEPT AS 
OTHERWISE INDICATED, THE INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT, INCLUDING THE INSIDE FRONT COVER PAGE AND THE APPENDICES 
ATTACHED HERETO, HAS BEEN OBTAINED FROM REPRESENTATIVES OF THE CITY, 
THE AIRPORT CONSULTANT  (AS DEFINED HEREIN), THE UNDERWRITERS AND 
FROM PUBLIC DOCUMENTS, RECORDS AND OTHER SOURCES CONSIDERED TO BE 
RELIABLE. 
THE UNDERWRITERS HAVE PROVIDED THE FOLLOWING SENTENCE FOR 
INCLUSION IN THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT: THE UNDERWRITERS HAVE REVIEWED 
THE INFORMATION IN THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT IN ACCORDANCE WITH, AND AS 
PART OF, THEIR RESPECTIVE RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THE FEDERAL SECURITIES 
LAWS AS APPLIED TO THE FACTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS TRANSACTION, 
BUT THE UNDERWRITERS DO NOT GUARANTEE THE ACCURACY OR 
COMPLETENESS OF SUCH INFORMATION. 
IN CONNECTION WITH THE OFFERING OF THE SERIES 2025  BONDS, THE 
UNDERWRITERS MAY OVERALLOT OR EFFECT TRANSACTIONS THAT STABILIZE 
OR MAINTAIN MARKET PRICES OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS AT LEVELS ABOVE 
THAT WHICH MIGHT OTHERWISE PREVAIL IN THE OPEN MARKET.  SUCH 
STABILIZING, IF COMMENCED, MAY BE DISCONTINUED AT ANY TIME. 
THE SERIES 2025 BONDS HAVE NOT BEEN REGISTERED WITH THE SEC 
UNDER THE SECURITIES ACT OF 1933, AS AMENDED, NOR HAS THE BOND 
ORDINANCE BEEN QUALIFIED UNDER THE TRUST INDENTURE ACT OF 1939, AS 
AMENDED, IN RELIANCE UPON CERTAIN EXEMPTIONS CONTAINED IN SUCH ACTS.  
THE REGISTRATION OR QUALIFICATION OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS IN 
ACCORDANCE WITH APPLICABLE PROVISIONS OF THE SECURITIES LAWS OF THE 
STATES, IF ANY, IN WHICH THE SERIES 2025 BONDS HAVE BEEN REGISTERED OR  
 
QUALIFIED AND THE EXEMPTION FROM REGISTRATION OR QUALIFICATION IN 
CERTAIN OTHER STATES CANNOT BE REGARDED AS A RECOMMENDATION 
THEREOF.  NEITHER THESE STATES NOR ANY OF THEIR AGENCIES HAVE PASSED 
UPON THE MERITS OF THE SERIES 2025  BONDS OR THE ACCURACY OR 
COMPLETENESS OF THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT.  ANY REPRESENTATION TO THE 
CONTRARY MAY BE A CRIMINAL OFFENSE. 
IN MAKING AN INVESTMENT DECISION, INVESTORS MUST RELY ON THEIR 
OWN EXAMINATION OF THE CITY, THE DEPARTMENT OF AVIATION (AS DEFINED 
HEREIN), THE AIRPORT (AS DEFINED HEREIN), AND THE TERMS OF THE OFFERING, 
INCLUDING THE MERITS AND RISKS INVOLVED.  THE SERIES 2025 BONDS HAVE 
NOT BEEN RECOMMENDED BY ANY FEDERAL OR STATE SECURITIES COMMISSION 
OR REGULATORY AUTHORITY.  ANY REPRESENTATION TO THE CONTRARY MAY 
BE A CRIMINAL OFFENSE. 
THE ORDER AND PLACEMENT OF INFORMATION IN THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT, INCLUDING THE INSIDE FRONT COVER PAGE AND THE APPENDICES 
ATTACHED HERETO, ARE NOT AN INDICATION OF RELEVANCE, MATERIALITY OR 
RELATIVE IMPORTANCE, AND THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT, INCLUDING THE INSIDE

ITS ENTIRETY.  THE CAPTIONS AND HEADINGS IN THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT ARE 
FOR CONVENIENCE ONLY AND IN NO WAY DEFINE, LIMIT OR DESCRIBE THE SCOPE 
OR INTENT, OR AFFECT THE MEANING OR CONSTRUCTION, OF ANY PROVISION OR 
SECTION IN THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT.

PURCHASERS IN EITHER BOUND OR PRINTED FORMAT ( "ORIGINAL BOUND

WWW.MUNIOS.COM.  THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT MAY BE RELIED ON ONLY IF IT 
IS IN ITS ORIGINAL BOUND FORMAT, OR IF IT IS PRINTED IN ITS ENTIRETY 
DIRECTLY FROM SUCH WEBSITE. 
REFERENCES TO WEBSITE ADDRESSES PRESENTED HEREIN, INCLUDING

INTRODUCTION 
General 
The purpose of this Official Statement, which includes the inside front cover page and the 
appendices attached hereto, is to provide certain information in connection with the issuance and 
sale by the City of Atlanta (the "City") of $49,490,000 in aggregate principal amount of its Airport 
General Revenue Bonds, Series 2025A (Non-AMT) (the "Series 2025A Bonds"), $914,640,000 in 
aggregate principal amount of its Airport General Revenue Bonds, Series 2025B-1 (AMT) (Green 
Bonds) (the "Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds") and $48,660,000 Airport General Revenue Bonds, 
Series 2025B-2 (AMT) (the "Series 2025B-2 Bonds" and, together with the Series 2025B-1 Green 
Bonds, the " Series 2025B Bonds").  The Series 2025A Bonds and the Series 2025B Bonds are 
hereinafter referred to as the "Series 2025 Bonds."  All capitalized terms used  herein and not 
otherwise expressly defined herein shall have the meanings assigned thereto in 
"APPENDIX C - DEFINITIONS OF CERTAIN TERMS" attached hereto. 
This Introduction is not a summary of this Official Statement and is intended only for quick 
reference.  It is only a brief description of and guide to, and is qualified in its entirety by reference 
to, the more complete and detailed information contained in the entire Official Statement, 
including the inside front cover page and the appendices attached hereto, and the documents 
summarized or described herein.  A full review should be made of the entire Official Statement, 
including the inside front cover page and the appendices attached hereto, and of the documents 
summarized or described herein.  The offering of the Series 2025 Bonds to potential investors is 
made only by means of the entire Official Statement.  No person is authorized to detach this 
Introduction from this Official Statement or to otherwise use it without the entire Official 
Statement, including the inside front cover page and the appendices attached hereto.  
2 
Authorization for the Series 2025 Bonds 
The Series 2025 Bonds will be issued pursuant to: (a) the Constitution and laws of the State 
of Georgia (the "State"), including specifically, but without limitation, Article  3 of Chapter 82 of 
Title 36 of the Official Code of Georgia Annotated, as amended, known as the " Revenue Bond 
Law"; (b) the Charter of the City of Atlanta, as amended (the " Charter"); and (c)  that certain 
Restated and Amended Master Bond Ordinance adopted by the City Council of the City (the "City 
Council") on March  20, 2000, as previously amended and supplemented (the " Master Bond 
Ordinance"), particularly as supplemented by that certain Thirty -Seventh Supplemental Bond 
Ordinance adopted by the City Council on June 2, 2025 and approved by the Mayor of the City 
(the "Mayor") on June 3, 2025, as amended and supplemented by that certain ordinance adopted 
by the City Council and approved by the Mayor on September 10, 2025 (together, the 
Thirty-Seventh Supplemental Bond Ordinance ").  The Master Bond Ordinance and the 
Thirty-Seventh Supplemental Bond Ordinance are hereinafter referred to as the "Bond Ordinance." 
Purpose of the Series 2025 Bonds 
The proceeds of the Series 2025 Bonds will be used for the purpose of providing funds to, 
among other things: (a) finance or refinance the costs of the planning, engineering, design, 
acquisition, equipping and construction of all or a portion of the 2025 Project (as defined herein), 
which constitutes a portion of the Capital Plan to 2031 (as defined herein); (b) fund deposits to the 
applicable subaccounts of the Debt Service Reserve Account to meet the Debt Service Reserve 
Requirement related to the Series 2025 Bonds; (c) pay capitalized interest; and (d) pay certain 
costs of issuance with respect to the Series 2025 Bonds.  See " PLAN OF FINANCE ," 
"ESTIMATED SOURCES AND USES OF FUNDS ," " SECURITY AND SOURCES OF 
PAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2025 BONDS - Debt Service Reserve Account," and "CAPITAL 
PLAN TO 2031" herein and " APPENDIX A - REPORT OF THE AIRPORT 
CONSULTANT - AIRPORT FACILITIES AND CAPITAL PLAN - Capital Improvement 
Plan - Capital Plan to 2031" attached hereto. 
Description of the Series 2025 Bonds 
The Series 2025 Bonds will be  issued as fully registered bonds, without coupons, in 
denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple thereof and initially will be registered in the 
name of Cede & Co., as nominee of The Depository Trust Company ( "DTC").  Purchases of 
beneficial ownership interests in the Series 2025 Bonds will be made in book-entry form only and 
purchasers will not receive physical delivery of bond certificates representing the beneficial 
ownership interests in the Series 2025 Bonds so purchased.  Payments of principal, of premium (if 
any), and interest on, any Series 2025 Bond will be made to Cede & Co., as nominee for DTC as 
registered owner of the Series 2025  Bonds, by U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association, 
as bond registrar (the "Bond Registrar") and paying agent (the "Paying Agent"), to be subsequently 
disbursed to the Beneficial Owners (as defined in APPENDIX  H attached hereto) of the Series 
2025 Bonds.  See " DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS - General" herein and 
"APPENDIX H - DTC AND THE BOOK-ENTRY ONLY SYSTEM" attached hereto. 
Interest on the Series 2025 Bonds is payable semiannually on January 1 and July 1 of each 
year, commencing January 1, 2026.  The Series 2025 Bonds will bear interest at the rates and will  
3 
be payable as to principal in the amounts and on the dates set forth on the inside front cover  page 
of this Official Statement.  See " DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2025  BONDS - General" 
herein. 
Certain of the Series 2025 Bonds may be subject to redemption prior to maturity as more 
fully described under the caption "DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS - Redemption 
Provisions" herein. 
Security and Sources of Payment for the Series 2025 Bonds 
The Series 2025 Bonds are special limited obligations of the City payable from and secured 
by a pledge of and Senior Lien on General Revenues on a parity with the Outstanding Senior Lien 
General Revenue Bonds (as defined herein) and any other Additional Bonds issued on a  parity 
with such Outstanding Senior Lien General Revenue Bonds under the Bond Ordinance, including, 
when and if issued, the Planned 2026-2031 General Revenue Bonds  (as defined herein) .  The 
Series 2025  Bonds will not be secured by PFC Revenues, Special Purpose Revenues or 
Released Revenues, any of which may be pledged to secure other bonds and obligations 
under the Bond Ordinance.  See "SECURITY AND SOURCES OF PAYMENT FOR THE 
SERIES 2025 BONDS - Series 2025 Bonds; Pledge of Pledged Revenues" and "OUTSTANDING 
AIRPORT OBLIGATIONS  - Senior Lien General Revenue Bonds " herein and 
"APPENDIX D - SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE BOND ORDINANCE " 
attached hereto. 
THE SERIES 2025 BONDS ARE SPECIAL LIMITED OBLIGATIONS OF THE 
CITY AND SHALL NOT BE DEEMED TO CONSTITUTE A DEBT OF THE CITY 
WITHIN THE MEANING OF ANY CONSTITUTIONAL LIMITATION ON DEBT NOR 
CONSTITUTE A PLEDGE OF THE FAITH AND CREDIT OF THE CITY.  THE SERIES 
2025 BONDS SHALL NOT BE PAYABLE FROM OR BE A CHARGE UPON ANY FUNDS 
OTHER THAN THE REVENUES AND AMOUNTS PLEDGED TO THE PAYMENT 
THEREOF PURSUANT TO THE BOND ORDINANCE, NOR SHALL THE CITY BE 
SUBJECT TO ANY PECUNIARY LIABILITY THEREON.  NO OWNER OR OWNERS 
OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS SHALL EVER HAVE THE RIGHT TO COMPEL ANY 
EXERCISE OF THE TAXING POWER OF THE CITY TO PAY THE SERIES 2025  
BONDS OR THE INTEREST THEREON, NOR TO ENFORCE PAYMENT OF THE 
SERIES 2025 BONDS AGAINST ANY PROPERTY OF THE CITY; NOR SHALL THE 
SERIES 2025 BONDS CONSTITUTE A CHARGE, LIEN, OR ENCUMBRANCE, LEGAL 
OR EQUITABLE, UPON ANY PROPERTY OF THE CITY, EXCEPT FOR THE 
AMOUNTS PLEDGED TO THE PAYMENT OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS AND ANY 
OTHER FUNDS PLEDGED TO SECURE THE PAYMENT OF THE SERIES 2025  
BONDS IN THE MANNER SET FORTH IN THE BOND ORDINANCE. 
The City and Airport Service Region 
The City is a municipal corporation of the State created by an act of the Georgia General 
Assembly (the "General Assembly") in 1843 and now existing and subject to the provisions of the 
Charter.  The City is the seat of government of the State and Fulton County, Georgia (the  
4 
"County").  The City's fiscal year is the 12 -month period ending on June  30 of each year (the 
"Fiscal Year").  See "THE CITY" herein. 
The primary service region of the Hartsfield -Jackson Atlanta International Airport (the 
"Airport") is the 8,474-square- mile, 29 -county Atlanta -Sandy Springs -Roswell metropolitan 
statistical area (the "Atlanta MSA").  According to the U.S. Department of Commerce, Bureau of 
the Census ( "Bureau of the Census "), the 2024 population of the Atlanta MSA was 6,411,000, 
accounting for approximately 57% of the State 's population and ranking the Atlanta MSA as the 
eighth largest metropolitan statistical area based on the estimated population for 2024.  The Atlanta 
MSA is the dominant population center and economic engine in the Airport 's combined primary 
and secondary service regions. 
The Airport's secondary service region is defined by the location of (and airline service 
provided at) the nearest airports.  The secondary service region includes the remainder of the State, 
as well as parts of Alabama, Tennessee, North Carolina, and South Carolina. 
See "THE AIRPORT - Airport Service Region" herein and "APPENDIX A - REPORT OF 
THE AIRPORT CONSULTANT  - ECONOMIC BASIS FOR AIRLINE TRAFFIC 
DEMAND - Airport Service Region" attached hereto. 
The Airport and Airport Facilities 
The Airport is located in Clayton and Fulton counties, Georgia, about 10 road miles south 
of downtown Atlanta, and is owned by the City and operated by the City's Department of Aviation 
(the "Department of Aviation").  The Airport is classified as a large hub by the Federal Aviation 
Administration (the "FAA") and is the principal commercial service airport serving the State and 
the southeastern United States.  The Airport serves as a primary transfer point in the national air 
transportation system, and among the airlines serving the Airport (the " Airlines") is the busiest 
hub for Delta Air Lines ( "Delta").  According to Airports Council International ( "ACI"), the 
Airport is the busiest passenger airport in the world with approximately 108.1 million total 
(enplaned and deplaned) passengers in calendar year 2024.  The Airport has five parallel east-west 
runways.  The central passenger terminal complex of the Airport (the " CPTC") was opened in 
1980 and originally consisted of a landside building (now the domestic terminal) and 
Concourses T-North, A, B, C, and D.  The CPTC has been expanded with the addition of the 
international landside terminal and Concourses T -South, E, and F to encompass approximately 
6.8 million square feet.  A 7,400-foot-long underground transportation mall accommodates an 
automated guideway transit system ( "AGTS"), known as the " Plane Train, " and pedestrian 
walkways that connect all terminal buildings and concourses.  The AGTS typically operates with 
260-person capacity, four -car trains at approximately two -minute intervals.   For additional 
information regarding the Airport, the Airport 's role in Delta 's system, and the facilities at the 
Airport, see " THE AIRPORT  - Airport Facilities " and " THE AIRPORT  - Airport's 
Role - Airport's Role in Delta's System" herein. 
Airport Use and Lease Agreement 
Most of the passenger and cargo airlines serving the Airport,  which collectively account 
for substantially all of the landed weight and passengers at the Airport, operate under the terms of  
5 
airport use and lease agreements (collectively, the "Airport Use and Lease Agreement").  Effective 
October 1, 2017, Airline rentals, fees, and charges were calculated in accordance with the 
procedures established under the Airport Use and Lease Agreement for calculating rentals, fees, 
and charges for the use and occupancy of facilities defining the two Airline cost centers, the 
Airfield Cost Center and the Terminal Cost Center (as those terms are defined in the Airport Use 
and Lease Agreement) . Provisions of the Airport Use and Lease Agreement governing the 
pre-approval of certain future capital projects included within the Approved Projects (as defined 
herein) and other provisions governing capital improvement projects took effect retroactively on 
July 1, 2016.  The Airport Use and  Lease Agreement superseded and replaced all outstanding 
airline agreements for the airfield and the CPTC.  Airlines that enter into an Airport Use and Lease 
Agreement are referred to as "Signatory Airlines."  For information regarding the term s of the 
Airport Use and Lease Agreement, including a discussion of certain approval rights of MII Eligible 
Signatory Airlines (as defined herein) for capital improvements to the Airfield Cost Center or the 
Terminal Cost Center, see "SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE AIRPORT USE 
AND LEASE AGREEMENT AND THE RATE MAKING METHODOLOGY - Term of Airport 
Use and Lease Agreement" herein.  
For more information related to the key provisions of the Airport Use and Lease 
Agreement, see " SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE AIRPORT USE AND 
LEASE AGREEMENT AND THE RATE MAKING METHODOLOGY" and "CAPITAL PLAN 
TO 2031" herein, and "APPENDIX A - REPORT OF THE AIRPORT CONSULTANT " and 
"APPENDIX E - SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE PRINCIPAL AIRPORT 
LEASES AND AGREEMENTS" attached hereto. 
Master Plan 
In May 2015, the City published a master plan to guide the long- term development of the 
Airport (the " Master Plan").  In May 2016, the City and the Signatory Airlines, as part of the 
Airport Use and Lease Agreement, mutually agreed to a $6.16 billion (in July 2014 dollars) 20-year 
plan of capital improvements consisting of the Approved Projects, and an agreed- upon funding 
plan for the Approved Projects.   
The Department of Aviation has initiated a process to update the Master Plan and currently 
expects to complete this  process by December 2025.  For information regarding the Approved 
Projects and other Airline -funded projects, see " SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF 
THE AIRPORT USE AND LEASE AGREEMENT AND THE RATE MAKING 
METHODOLOGY - Approved Projects" herein. 
Capital Plan to 2031 
The City has developed an approximately $12.6 billion capital improvement plan at the 
Airport, which the City expects to fund through Fiscal Year 2031 (the "Capital Plan to 2031" ).  
The Capital Plan to 2031 includes (a) certain of the Approved Projects, (b) certain projects agreed 
to by the City and the MII Eligible Signatory Airlines through the majority- in-interest ("MII") 
approval process subsequent to the date of the Airport Use and Lease Agreement, and (c) Exempt 
Projects (as defined in the Airport Use  and Lease Agreement).  Only projects in the Capital Plan 
to 2031 are considered in the Report of the Airport Consultant.  See "CAPITAL PLAN TO 2031"  
6 
and "CERTAIN FACTORS AFFECTING THE AIRPORT AND THE AIR TRANSPORTATION 
INDUSTRY AND OTHER CONSIDERATIONS  - Capacity of the Airport " herein and 
"APPENDIX A - REPORT OF THE AIRPORT CONSULTANT" attached hereto. 
Report of the Airport Consultant 
In its capacity as airport consultant to the City, LeighFisher (the "Airport Consultant") has 
prepared its report dated August 20, 2025 (the "Report of the Airport Consultant ") in connection 
with the issuance of the Series 2025 Bonds , which report is attached hereto as 
"APPENDIX A - REPORT OF THE AIRPORT CONSULTANT. "  The Report of the Airport 
Consultant provides, among other things, a summary of the funding plan for the Capital Plan to 
2031, analyses of historical airline service and passenger traffic  and key factors that may affect 
future airline travel, analyses of historical Airport revenues and expenses, and financial forecasts 
demonstrating the sufficiency of Net General Revenues and PFC Revenues, as applicable, to pay 
the Debt Service Requirements of the General Revenue Bonds and Hybrid Bonds set forth in the 
Report of the Airport Consultant, including, but not limited to, the Series 2025 Bonds and, when 
and if issued, the Planned 2026-2031 General Revenue Bonds, while meeting the debt service 
coverage requirements of the rate covenant under the Bond Ordinance.  For additional information 
on historical and forecast Revenues, see " REPORT OF THE AIRPORT CONSULTANT AND 
RATE COVENANT FORECAST " herein and " APPENDIX A - REPORT OF THE AIRPORT 
CONSULTANT" attached hereto. 
Designation of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds 
The City has designated the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds (as defined 
herein) based upon, among other things, the Green Bond Framework (as defined herein) and the 
Second Party Opinion (as defined herein) provided by Kestrel (the " External Reviewer"), all as 
more particularly described under " DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS 
GREEN BONDS" herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL" 
attached hereto. 
Continuing Disclosure 
In order to assist the Underwriters (as defined herein) in complying with paragraph (b)(5) 
of Rule  15c2-12 of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the " SEC") promulgated 
pursuant to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (the " Exchange Act"), as in effect on the date 
hereof ("Rule 15c2-12"), simultaneously with the issuance of the Series 2025  Bonds, the City, as 
an "obligated person" under Rule 15c2-12, will enter into a Continuing Disclosure Agreement (the 
"Continuing Disclosure Agreement ") with Digital Assurance Certification, L .L.C. ("DAC"), as 
initial disclosure dissemination agent, under which the City will undertake to provide continuing 
disclosure with respect to the Series 2025 Bonds and the Airport for the benefit of the holders of 
the Series 2025 Bonds. 
See "CONTINUING DISCLOSURE " herein and " APPENDIX G  - FORM OF 
CONTINUING DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT" attached hereto.

Other Information 
This Official Statement speaks only as of its date and the information contained herein is 
subject to change.  This Official Statement , including the inside front cover page  and the 
appendices attached hereto , contains brief descriptions of, among other matters, the City, the 
Airport, the Series 2025 Bonds, the security and sources of payment for the Series 2025 Bonds, 
the Bond Ordinance, and the Airport Use and Lease Agreement.  Such descriptions and 
information do not purport to be comprehensive or definitive.  The summaries of various 
constitutional provisions, statutes, the Bond Ordinance, the Airport Use and Lease Agreement, and 
other documents are intended as summaries only and are qualified in their entirety by reference to 
such documents, and references herein to the Series 2025 Bonds are qualified in their entirety to 
the forms thereof included in the Bond Ordinance.  Copies of the Bond Ordinance, the Airport Use 
and Lease Agreement, and other relevant documents and information are available, upon written 
request and payment of a charge for copying, mailing and handling, from the Chief Financial 
Officer, Department of Finance, 68 Mitchell Street, S.W., Suite  11100, South Tower, Atlanta, 
Georgia 30303, telephone (404) 330-6430. 
PLAN OF FINANCE 
The proceeds of the Series 2025 Bonds will be used for the purpose of providing funds to, 
among other things: (a) finance or refinance the costs of the planning, engineering, design, 
acquisition, equipping and construction of all or a portion of the 2025 Project, which constitutes a 
portion of the Capital Plan to 2031; (b) fund deposits to the applicable subaccounts of the Debt 
Service Reserve Account to meet the Debt Service Reserve Requirement related to the Series 2025 
Bonds; (c) pay capitalized interest; and (d) pay certain costs of issuance with respect to the Series 
2025 Bonds.  See "ESTIMATED SOURCES AND USES OF FUNDS ," and "SECURITY AND 
SOURCES OF PAYMENT FOR  THE SERIES 2025 BONDS - Debt Service Reserve Account " 
herein. 
The "2025 Project" is comprised of certain projects from the Capital Plan to 2031, as more 
fully described in the Thirty-Seventh Supplemental Bond Ordinance, including, but not limited to, 
all or a portion of the projects described below. 
Replace 2003 Steam Boilers at The Main Terminal Central Utility Plant .  The Main 
Terminal Central Utility Plant (TCUP) provides chilled and hot water to the Domestic Terminal 
and Concourses T-D.  This project replaced steam boilers last installed in 2003 with a new, more 
efficient heating hot water generation system.  Six modular dual-fuel high-efficiency heating hot 
water generation systems along with support systems required to maintain a fully functional and 
compliant heating hot water generation system were installed.   A portion of the proceeds of the 
Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds will be used to finance or refinance those components of this project 
that are eligible green projects.  See "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS 
GREEN BONDS" herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL" 
attached hereto. 
North Terminal Master Plan Enabling Work.  For the first phase of the Domestic Terminal 
North Master Plan program (enabling work), some airport stakeholders and service providers that  
8 
were currently housed on the north exterior wall of the Domestic Terminal North Ticketing lobby 
are being relocated to newly renovated spaces within the terminal and Atrium area.  This consists 
of Airline Ticket offices, Baggage Service Offices and Department of Aviation management 
offices.  The North Terminal Master plan project has subsequent phases that will replace and add 
airline counter positions, increase lobby circulation, modernize 3 Gates in Concourse T (T9-T11), 
build out shell space for 2 Airline Clubs and relocate existing concession space.  The project is 
expected to be completed by mid-year 2030. 
Concourse T-North Existing Gates Modernization (T12-T15) .  Four of the existing gates 
of T -North Extension (T12 - T15) were modernized to meet the new design standards for 
holdrooms, concourse corridors, and exterior cladding/glazing.  Additionally, this project replaced 
the existing roof portions above the scope area; as well as the extents between Gate T3 to T4 above 
the T3 Distributed Antenna System Space.  A portion of the proceeds of the Series 2025B-1 Green 
Bonds will be used to finance or refinance those components of this project that are eligible green 
projects.  See "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B -1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS" 
herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL" attached hereto. 
Ramp 6 North Domestic Gates (Conc ourse E).  This project constructed three additional 
Group III capable gates at Ramp 6 North.  This expansion is part of an overall effort to maximize 
gating capability and to enable the construction for the Concourse D Widening project.  The 
expansion is a 32,000 square foot two-story facility connected to Concourse E by a new elevated 
bridge.  The boarding level will contain holdrooms, concessions and restrooms.  The apron level 
will contain areas for airline operations and mechanical / electrical support systems.  The existing 
apron will be extended east to accommodate aircraft parking and fueling.  A portion of the proceeds 
of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds will be used to finance or refinance those components of this 
project that are eligible green projects.  See "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B -1 GREEN 
BONDS AS GREEN BONDS" herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM 
KESTREL" attached hereto. 
Fire Life Safety Enhancements to AGTS and Domestic Terminal .  Installing upgrades to 
fire safety equipment and structures in the Transportation Mall, including the AGTS all to comply 
with National Fire Protection Association life safety code standards. 
Grease Interceptors at Gates B33, C10, and F12.  Installing four new underground 
1,500-gallon grease interceptors and one new above ground 3000- gallon grease interceptor to 
support the Airport 's expansion of Airport food and beverage operations. The scope of work 
needed to install these grease interceptors will include site layout, utility identification, excavation, 
and backfill, congregate base and pavement work, installation of new grease int erceptors, 
installation of bent popping, connection to existing sanitary lines, and pavement markings.  
Concourse D Widening.  Concourse D, one of the four original concourses that opened in 
1980 and is undersized, is being widened and renovated to bring it up to the space and amenity 
standards at the other concourses.  Concourse D is being widened from 60 feet to 99 feet and 
extended by approximately 288 feet to add approximately 100,000 square feet of space at the 
boarding level.  The depth of the holdrooms will be increased from 20 feet to 35 feet and provide 
additional space for circulation, restrooms, and concessions.  Apron and gate layouts will be 
modified to provide 34 gates, all configured to accommodate Airplane Design Group ("ADG") III  
9 
aircraft.  The existing concourse provides 40 gates, 21 of which can accommodate ADG III aircraft.  
Concourse exteriors and interiors will be modernized to match the other concourses, and vertical 
circulation and utility systems will be replaced and upgraded.  The Concourse D widening project 
also  included the construction of an approximately 25,000 square feet of space for a Delta Sky 
Club above the boarding level (as further described below).  The Concourse D widening project is 
being constructed in phases scheduled to be completed in 2026 through 2029.  A portion of the 
proceeds of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds will be used to finance or refinance those components 
of this project that are eligible green projects.  See "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B -1 
GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS" herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION 
FROM KESTREL" attached hereto. 
Concourse D Sky Club Core and Shell.  The 2025 Project included the Concourse D Sky 
Club. The New Concourse D Sky Club opened in April, 2025 and consists of approximately 25,000 
square feet of new concourse infrastructure and building that will be exclusively leased to Delta 
for use as a Sky Club.  The core building shell and exterior included a condition of usable square 
footage in a leasable dry condition to allow Delta to complete the Tenant Improvements.  To create 
the shell space, a design, management, and construction effort took place, which include d the 
demolition, removal, or relocation of existing obstructions, enabling efforts to create the required 
access to the new club, abatement or removal of hazardous materials, construction of foundations, 
structural members, exterior walls, windows, a level floor, vertical circulation between floors, a 
finished roof including all penetrations, a mechanical room, egress stairs, procurement and 
installation of the HVAC unit(s), the HVAC main trunk line(s), fire suppression system, main 
electrical panel, all required connections to the airport or AATC controlled communication rooms 
or other building monitoring systems, airport security systems, all life safety building systems; and 
all testing and commissioning of building support systems.  A portion of the proceeds of the Series 
2025B-1 Green Bonds will be used to finance or refinance those components of this project that 
are eligible green projects.  See "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B -1 GREEN BONDS AS 
GREEN BONDS" herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL" 
attached hereto. 
Concourse D Sky Club - Boarding Level.  The 2025 Project also include d the demolition 
and reconstruction of the nose pocket  of the Concourse D Sky Club at column lines 44 to 40 to 
create a linear expansion joint at column line 41. The design elements include d core and shell, 
mechanical, electrical, and plumbing, and full interior build- out of the main corridor, holdrooms, 
circulation, public restrooms, concessions, and other utility rooms completed as " shell" ready 
spaces with utility point of connections provided within each space.  The work at Concourse  D 
Sky Club also included: the construction and/or installation of temporary holdrooms, temporary 
corridor(s) to facilitate passenger circulation around construction, temporary emergency egress, 
temporary utilities, temporary signage, the modification of existing passenger boarding bridges, 
the demolition of the existing Concourse D boarding-level and roof-level columns, and the existing 
restroom. The Concourse D Sky Club portion of the 2025 Project has been completed.  A portion 
of the proceeds of the Series 2025B -1 Green Bonds will be used to finance or refinance those 
components of this project that are eligible green projects.  See "DESIGNATION OF SERIES 
2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS" herein and "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY 
OPINION FROM KESTREL" attached hereto.  
10 
ATL SkyTrain .  The 2025 Project will also include an upgrade and revamping of the 
SkyTrain On- Board and Wayside a utomatic train control system and equipment , the p ower 
distribution system, platform screen doors, guideway, communication s ystems and existing f leet 
systems. Some of the existing vehicles will be refurbished and replaced, and the Airport plans on 
purchasing additional vehicles.  
Terminal (CPTC) Station.  The mechanical, electrical, plumbing and conveyance systems 
in the CPTC Station will be refreshed and the Airport plans on this work being included in the 
2025 Project. The scope of the mechanical work includes electrical heaters, exhaust hood, remote 
thermal units, supply fans, grounding system, heat pump, uninterruptible power supply, and fan 
coils. The scope of the electrical work includes but is not limited to modernizing lighting, replacing 
mounted electrical fixtures, and fire alarm panels.  The scope of the plumbing work includes 
replacing the drinking fountain and mop sink.  The scope of the 2025 Project relating to the 
conveyance systems includes refreshing and/or replacing four escalators and four elevators. 
Miscellaneous work includes but is not limited to roofing, sliding, doors, exterior windows, 
wayfinding signs and station refresh.  
See "CAPITAL PLAN TO 2031" and " SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF

DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS 
Certain information under this caption relating to the Green Bond Principles (June 2025 
with June 2022 Appendix 1) (the "Green Bond Principles ") established by the International 
Capital Market Association ("ICMA"), has been obtained from ICMA's website.  The information 
under the captions "Definition of Green Bonds Pursuant to ICMA Green Bond Principles," 
"Independent Second Party Opinion on the Green Bond Framework and Disclaimers," and 
"External Reviewer" below and the Second Party Opinion have been obtained from the External 
Reviewer.  None o f the City, the Underwriters, the Financial Advisors, Co- Bond Counsel, 
Co-Disclosure Counsel, or Co-Underwriters Counsel: (a) has independently confirmed or verified 
all of the information under this caption; (b) has assumed any obligation to ensure that the Series 
2025 Green Bonds comply with any legal or other standards or principles that may be related to 
the Green Bonds designation discussed below, including the Green Bond Principles; and (c) make 
any representation or warranty or take any responsibilit y for the accuracy, completeness, or 
reliability of all of the information under this caption. 
General 
The City has designated the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds based upon, 
among other things, (a) the Green Bond Framework, as more particularly described under 
"DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS - City of Atlanta 
Department of Aviation Green Bond Framework" below; (b) the Second Party Opinion provided 
by the External Reviewer, as more particularly described under " DESIGNATION OF SERIES  
11 
2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS  - Independent Second Party Opinion on the 
Green Bond Framework and Disclaimers; " and (c) the information from the sources indicated in 
the Green Bond Framework and the Second Party Opinion and the assumptions contained therein.  
For additional information relating to the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds and the proposed use of 
the proceeds of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds, see " PLAN OF FINANCE " herein and 
"APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL" attached hereto. 
The City's obligation to make payments with respect to the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds 
is not conditioned on the completion of any particular project, the satisfaction of any condition 
relating to any eligible Green Projects (as described in the Green Bond Principles and the Second 
Party Opinion), the satisfaction of any condition relating to eligible green projects as described in 
the Green Bond Framework, or the designation of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green 
Bonds. 
The designation by the City of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds does not 
entitle the owner of any Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds to any benefit under the Code (as defined 
herein) or under the Bond Ordinance nor does such designation entitle the owners of the Series 
2025B-1 Green Bonds to any additional security beyond that described under "SECURITY AND 
SOURCES OF PAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2025 BONDS" herein and 
"APPENDIX D - SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE BOND ORDINANCE " 
attached hereto. 
Unanticipated events and circumstances may occur which impact the accuracy of the 
information and assumptions in the Green Bond Framework, the Second Party Opinion, the 
information from the sources indicated in the Green Bond Framework and the Second Party  
Opinion, and/or necessitate changes to such information and assumptions.  The City cannot 
guarantee that any such events or circumstances will not affect the continuing qualification of the 
Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds in the future.  The City has no obligation to ensure 
compliance with any legal or other principles relating to Green Bonds, including as such principles 
may evolve over time, and any noncompliance with such principles will not constitute a default 
under the Bond Ordinance.  See " DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS 
GREEN BONDS - Certain Factors Affecting the Green Bonds Market and Other Considerations" 
below. 
Definition of Green Bonds Pursuant to ICMA Green Bond Principles 
The Green Bond Principles define " Green Bonds" as: any type of bond instrument where 
the proceeds or an equivalent amount will be exclusively applied to finance or re -finance, in part 
or in full, new and/or existing eligible Green Projects and which are aligned with the four core 
components of the Green Bond Principles.  The four core components for alignment with the Green 
Bond Principles are: (a) use of proceeds; (b) process for project evaluation and selection; 
(c) management of proceeds; and (d) reporting.  Additional information regarding the Green Bond 
Principles is available on ICMA's website.  
12 
City of Atlanta Department of Aviation Green Bond Framework 
The City of Atlanta Department of Aviation Green Bond Framework dated July 2024 (the 
"Green Bond Framework") was developed by the Department of Aviation to, among other things, 
document certain processes and commitments relating to the issuance and designation of bonds as 
Green Bonds by the City for the financing or refinancing of eligible green projects at the Airport 
(the "Department of Aviation Green Bond Program ").  The Green Bond Framework includes a 
description of, among other things, (a) the approach to sustainability at the Airport; (b) the process 
for evaluating and selecting eligible green projects under the Department of Aviation Green Bond 
Program; and (c) the management of the net proceeds of Green Bonds issued under the Department 
of Aviation Green Bond Program, including the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds.  For information 
regarding certain voluntary reporting relating to the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds, see 
"DESIGNATION OF SERIES 2025B-1 GREEN BONDS AS GREEN BONDS  - Voluntary 
Reporting" below. 
A copy of the Green Bond Framework is available on the website for the Airport at: 
https://www.atl.com/business-information/investor-relations. 
Independent Second Party Opinion on the Green Bond Framework and Disclaimers 
In connection with the proposed designation of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green 
Bonds, the City engaged the External Reviewer to deliver an independent second party opinion on 
the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds for alignment with the Green Bond Principles and the Green 
Bond Framework, which is attached hereto as APPENDIX  I (the "Second Party Opinion").  The 
External Reviewer has determined that the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds conform with the Green 
Bond Framework and the four core components of the Green Bond Principles, and are eligible for 
Green Bonds designation, all as more fully described in the Second Party Opinion.  See 
"APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM KESTREL." 
The Second Party Opinion issued by the External Reviewer does not and is not intended to 
make any representation or give any assurance with respect to any other matter relating to the 
Green Bond Framework, the Department of Aviation Green Bond Program, or any bonds, 
including the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds, issued under the Green Bond Framework.  The Second 
Party Opinion provided by the External Reviewer is not a recommendation to any person to 
purchase, hold, or sell the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds and the Green Bond designation assigned 
to the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds does not address the market price or suitability of the Series 
2025B-1 Green Bonds for a particular investor and does not and is not in any way intended to 
address the likelihood of timely payment of interest or principal on the Series 2025B-1 Green 
Bonds when due. 
In issuing the Second Party Opinion, the External Reviewer has assumed and relied upon 
the accuracy and completeness of the information made publicly available by the City or that was 
otherwise made available to the External Reviewer. 
The Second Party Opinion is not a guarantee of alignment nor warrants any alignment with 
future versions of the Green Bond Principles or relevant market standards.  
13 
The Second Party Opinion is included as APPENDIX  I attached hereto in reliance upon 
the knowledge and experience of the External Reviewer as an independent external reviewer in 
connection with Green Bonds.  For additional information regarding the External  Reviewer, see 
"External Reviewer " below and " APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION FROM 
KESTREL" attached hereto. 
The Second Party Opinion should be read in its entirety for an understanding of the 
External Reviewer 's qualifications, assumptions and rationale underlying the findings, 
determinations and conclusions of the External Reviewer and the City's designation of the Series 
2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds.  See "APPENDIX I - SECOND PARTY OPINION 
FROM KESTREL" attached hereto. 
External Reviewer 
For over 23 years, the External Reviewer has been consulting in sustainable finance.  The 
External Reviewer is an " Approved Verifier" accredited by the Climate Bonds Initiative  and the 
market leader for Second Party opinions in US Public Finance .  The External Reviewer reviews 
transactions in all asset classes worldwide for alignment with the ICMA Green Bond Principles, 
Social Bond Principles, Sustainability Bond Guidelines and the Climate Bonds Initiative Standards 
and Criteria.   
Voluntary Reporting 
The information relating to the use of the proceeds of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds, or 
the designation of the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds by the City is not required 
under paragraph (b)(5) of Rule 15c2-12.  Accordingly, the City will not undertake to provide any 
continuing disclosure with respect to such information pursuant to the Continuing Disclosure 
Agreement.  See "CONTINUING DISCLOSURE" herein. 
Notwithstanding the foregoing, the City intends to voluntarily file an annual report with 
certain information relating to the Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds.  Such information may be 
included in its annual Environmental, Social and Governance Report for the Airport or any other 
manner as determined necessary or appropriate by the City.  See " THE 
AIRPORT - Environmental, Social & Governance" herein. 
Certain Factors Affecting the Green Bonds Market and Other Considerations 
The information in this section describes certain factors affecting the market for Green 
Bonds and other considerations which may impact the designation of the Series 2025B-1 Green 
Bonds as Green Bonds.  The following discussion is not meant to be an exhaustive list of the factors 
and other considerations affecting the Green Bonds market or impacting the designation of the 
Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as Green Bonds and does not necessarily reflect the relative 
importance of the various factors and considerations.  In addition, there can be no assurance that 
other factors or considerations not discussed herein are not and/or will not become relevant or 
material in the future.  Investors are advised to consider the following factors along with all other 
information described in the Second Party Opinion, this Official Statement or incorporated by 
reference herein when evaluating the City 's designation of the  Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds as 
Green Bonds to obtain information essential to the making of an informed investment decision.  
14 
The environmental, social and governance ( "ESG") finance market, which includes the 
market for Green Bonds, is rapidly evolving and various factors relating to the market for Green 
Bonds may be relevant to the investment decisions of potential investors.  Such factors include, 
but are not limited to, (a) competing and inconsistent industry practices; (b)  anti-ESG legislation, 
regulations and pronouncements by State and local governments; (c)  inconsistent legal, 
regulatory, and policy expectations; (d) inconsistent identification of green bond factors or 
principles considered by market participants, including issuers, credit analysts, investors, rating 
agencies, and external reviewers; (e) inconsistent identification of factors relating to Green Bonds 
and the nexus to credit and materiality; (f) disparity in the standards for labeling or designating 
bonds as Green Bonds; (g) availability, quality, and comparability of reporting relating to Green 
Bonds; (h) potential failure to achieve expected impacts of projects relating to Green Bonds; and 
(i) "greenwashing risk," which is the risk that the proceeds of the sale of a bond marketed as a 
Green Bond will not be applied to the appropriate projects.

DESCRIPTION OF THE SERIES 2025 BONDS 
General 
The Series 2025 Bonds will be dated the date of their issuance and delivery and will bear 
interest from the dated date thereof at the rates set forth on the inside front cover page of this 
Official Statement, payable semiannually on January  1 and July 1 of each year, commencing 
January 1, 2026. 
The Series 2025 Bonds will be  issued as fully registered bonds, without coupons, in 
denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple thereof and initially will be registered in the 
name of Cede & Co., as nominee of DTC.  Purchases of beneficial ownership interests in the Series 
2025 Bonds will be made in book-entry form only and purchasers will not receive physical delivery 
of bond certificates representing the beneficial ownership interests in the Series 2025 Bonds so 
purchased.  Payments of principal of, premium, if any, and interest on, any Series 2025 Bond will 
be made to Cede & Co., as nominee for DTC as registered owner of the Series 2025 Bonds, by 
U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association, the Bond Registrar and Paying Agent, to be 
subsequently disbursed to the Beneficial Owners of the Series 2025 Bonds.  If the book- entry 
system is discontinued, the Series 2025 Bonds will be delivered as described in the Thirty-Seventh 
Supplemental Bond Ordinance and Beneficial Owners will become the registered owners of the 
Series 2025 Bonds .  See " APPENDIX H - DTC AND THE BOOK- ENTRY ONLY SYSTEM " 
attached hereto. 
Redemption Provisions 
Optional Redemption.   The Series 2025A Bonds maturing on or before July 1, 2034 may 
not be called for optional redemption prior to maturity.  The Series 2025A Bonds maturing on 
or after  July 1, 20 35 are subject to optional redemption prior to maturity at the option of the 
City on or after July 1 , 2034, in whole or in part at any time in the manner and subject to the 
provisions of the Bond Ordinance, at a redemption price equal to the principal amount thereof, 
together with accrued interest to the redemption date and without premium. 
The Series 2025B-1 Green Bonds maturing on or before July  1, 2034 may not be called 
for optional redemption prior to maturity.  The Series 202 5B-1 Green Bonds maturing on or 
after July 1, 2035 are subject to optional redemption prior to maturity at the option of the City 
on or after July 1 , 20 34, in whole or in part at any time in the manner and subject to the 
provisions of the Bond Ordinance, at a redemption price equal to the principal amount thereof, 
together with accrued interest to the redemption date and without premium.  
The Series 2025B -2 Bonds maturing on or before July 1, 2034  may not be called for 
optional redemption prior to maturity.  The Series 2025B -2 Bonds maturing on or after July 1, 
2035 are subject to optional redemption prior to maturity at the option of the City on or after 
July 1, 2034, in whole or in part at any time in the manner and subject to the provisions of the 
Bond Ordinance, at a redemption price equal to the principal amount thereof, together with 
accrued interest to the redemption date and without premium.
